completion_scope: CREATE ROLE postgres NOLOGIN succeeded and survived recreate; authorized override recreate executed; host 127.0.0.1:55432 remained unreachable because foundation_data network is internal:true; migrations not re-applied; T054 incomplete

runtime_result.root_cause: Postgres is attached only to foundation_data (platform-foundation-local-data) which is defined with internal: true in compose.yaml; Docker does not publish host ports for containers solely on internal networks. Override port mapping is present in HostConfig but cannot become active.

failure.assessment: Authorized CREATE ROLE postgres NOLOGIN succeeded. Authorized override recreate left Postgres healthy with role and disposable DB intact, but 127.0.0.1:55432 is unreachable because compose.yaml marks foundation_data as internal:true and Postgres is not attached to a non-internal network. No topology change, migration re-apply, or second recreate was performed after this failed verification.
failure.recovery: Preserve container, volumes, role, DB, and restored migrations. Obtain an exact HCP-04 amendment authorizing one of: (A) attach postgres also to foundation_edge for publishability while keeping foundation_data internal; (B) set foundation_data.internal to false for local disposable use; or (C) amend the HCP-03 harness/attestation to use docker-exec transport without host port 55432. Then re-verify port or approved alternate transport, re-apply migrations, attest, and finish T054 gates.

result.next_required_action: Exact HCP-04 amendment to unblock host publish or authorize docker-exec-only HCP-03 transport
